rejected_candidates.reason: reference-guided vocal/dynamics moves reduced MuQ reward versus agent-rock

learning: For this rock multitrack, MuQ-A1 preferred the full project agent/knowledge/AutoFOH/phase stack with large-system-polish disabled and rejected both source-rules-only simplification and extra drum-focus boosts. Treat large-system-polish and drum-focus pushes as candidates to A/B, not defaults, in future MuQ-led offline rock passes.

accepted_actions_summary: AutoFXPlanner shared filtered vocal plate, short drum room, ducked tempo delay, and chorus doubler
reference-guided vocal FX focus
section-selective subtle modulation/space on low-MuQ windows 63-102s, 128-166s, 178-202s
master conformed to -14 LUFS with no clipping

rejected_candidates.reason: control; lower than reference-guided FX focus
default FX improved little and left a very low 0.396 window
section EQ alone underperformed section modulation

learning: MuQ-A1 rewarded subtle spatial/modulation FX and reference-guided vocal space, but did not reward heavier dynamics ride or broad post-master EQ. For SONG REPA, a 0.9 whole-mix MuQ gate is above the provided commercial reference score; future gates should be calibrated against reference scores before treating 0.9 as required.

accepted_actions_summary: AYAIC Mix Monolith-inspired level-plane and relative-balance workflow
layer-group balancing before loudness
drum phase alignment and overhead-aware panning
bounded EQ/compression from project rules and measured analysis
shared filtered FX returns: vocal plate, drum room, tempo delay, modulation doubler
real MuQ-A1 audit with fallback disabled
final static trim only; no final limiter, no reference mastering, no extra compression
rejected_actions_summary: MuQ score chasing with segment-by-segment tonal changes
clipping or distortion to raise loudness
learning: For Desktop/MIX, the operator accepted the AYAIC-style soft-print direction even though its MuQ-A1 mean was lower than previous metric-chasing renders. Future passes for this material should prioritize stable balance, open dynamics, filtered FX depth, and light master trim over maximizing MuQ with aggressive compression or abrupt tonal changes.

accepted_actions_summary: strict MuQ-A1 gate used as hard critic with fallback disabled
candidate search around prior MuQ-preferred FX15 section-modulation render

rejected_actions_summary: forcing score over 0.78 with aggressive EQ
learning: Strict MuQ-A1 control around a calibrated 0.78 gate produced useful SONG REPA results. The best near-threshold safe direction was FX15 section-selective modulation plus slight high-air softening. Use this render as the baseline for future MuQ-led SONG REPA passes, but do not treat the missing 0.0013 as permission for destructive mastering; continue only with small reversible candidates and preserve headroom.

learning: This exact Mixing Station VIS FX15/Air -0.80 render is the golden SONG REPA baseline. Future SONG REPA and similar rock passes should start by reproducing this recipe or using it as candidate_000_no_change, then add AYAIC balance and new decision-layer candidates only as reversible tests. Safety Governor must preserve no_change unless a candidate clearly beats the golden baseline after loudness-matched criticism.
